_meta.attribution: Drug label data courtesy of the U.S. National Library of Medicine, DailyMed.
_meta.note: Descriptive context only, not regulatory advice. A label counts only when it carries an FDA-approved marketing category (NDA/ANDA/BLA); DailyMed also indexes dietary supplements, homeopathics, medical foods, and OTC-monograph products, which are NOT FDA-approved drugs and are excluded. label_relationship: on_label = the condition appears in the FDA-approved label Indications & Usage section (an approved use); off_label = the drug has an FDA-approved label but the condition is not an approved indication; no_fda_label = no FDA-approved drug label found (supplement, homeopathic, OTC-monograph product, or non-US/unapproved). "Approved" means FDA-approved (US); non-US approvals are out of scope.
